# Fly Me To The Moon
Category: Forensics, 85 Points

## Description

> Our Satellite managed to pick up some data from outer space (Aliens? wink wink). 
> 
> We have tried to make some sense of it for a long time, Maybe you can? 
> 
> It`s a new year after all...


## Solution

Let's check the file that was attached:

```console
root@kali:/media/sf_CTFs/matrix/Fly_Me_To_The_Moon# file message.gz
message.gz: data
```

It has a `.gz` extension, but isn't a GZip file.

```console
root@kali:/media/sf_CTFs/matrix/Fly_Me_To_The_Moon# cat message.gz | xxd -g 1  | head
00000000: 15 14 13 12 00 00 00 00 2b c8 e7 17 00 6e 79 6b  ........+....nyk
00000010: 72 71 45 4d 4b 77 2b 65 57 67 6b 42 45 54 71 67  rqEMKw+eWgkBETqg
00000020: 7a 42 46 30 58 75 49 68 74 53 4b 56 46 53 6d 73  zBF0XuIhtSKVFSms
00000030: 41 54 73 55 6f 51 6c 4a 38 37 49 59 6e 5a 53 39  ATsUoQlJ87IYnZS9
00000040: 6a 4a 64 31 15 14 13 12 01 00 00 00 22 50 e4 4c  jJd1........"P.L
00000050: 00 47 46 5a 79 64 39 68 62 35 5a 57 79 72 6d 48  .GFZyd9hb5ZWyrmH
00000060: 65 69 2f 6d 4c 53 47 74 56 34 62 76 6c 5a 6d 73  ei/mLSGtV4bvlZms
00000070: 54 57 6b 6f 61 49 69 39 7a 34 68 74 4a 58 63 4c  TWkoaIi9z4htJXcL
00000080: 79 42 43 78 6f 66 73 2b 15 14 13 12 02 00 00 00  yBCxofs+........
00000090: 44 be 79 53 00 2f 71 56 31 7a 6f 7a 52 4c 70 6b  D.yS./qV1zozRLpk
```

It looks like part binary, part base64. However the base64 doesn't mean anything.

We can see sort of a pattern, let's try to align the hex dump to make it clearer:

What this looks like is:
```c
typedef struct
{
   uint32_t magic;    // Equal to 0x12131415
   uint32_t id;       // Running index
   uint32_t checksum; // Maybe a checksum of an unknown format
   uint8_t  bool_val; // Unknown
   uint8_t  msg[37];  // Message
}
```
